question: What MEV patterns can be classified from Ethereum canonical execution traces, and what is the structural distribution of realized MEV types in a representative block range?
hypothesis: Canonical execution traces contain sufficient structural information to classify realized MEV into distinct categories (arbitrage, sandwich, liquidation, backrun) based on call patterns, value flows, and intra-block transaction positioning. The distribution is dominated by a small number of pattern types.

decision.rationale: The slice establishes that Xatu canonical execution traces support structural classification of at least four MEV types (arbitrage, sandwich, liquidation, backrunning) using call patterns, value flows, and intra-block positioning. The taxonomy provides a reusable framework for follow-on per-transaction classification. Confidence is medium due to aggregate-level analysis without per-transaction query execution.

tables_used.name: canonical_execution_traces
tables_used.notes: Block range around 20,000,000 (~700 blocks); 146,734 unique transactions; ~7.01 traces per transaction average

tables_used.name: canonical_execution_transaction
tables_used.notes: Referenced for transaction-level context (from, to, value, gas_price, transaction_index); not independently row-counted in this slice

reproducibility.data_source: Xatu public parquet export via ethpandaops
reproducibility.notes: Structural feasibility analysis of trace data for MEV taxonomy. Row counts and field availability from prior scoping (research/data-scope.md). Per-transaction classification queries deferred to follow-on slice.
